revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> {{for|searching instead for page titles that '''begin''' with a word or part of a word|Template:Look from}} {{high risk}}
== Usage == In a See also section, use the following wikitext to create a link to a search for page titles which contain the given word or phrase: <nowiki />* {<nowiki />{ In title | ''title phrase'' | ''label'' }}
The default behavior of this template is searching for the entire [[Wikipedia:Pagename|pagename]] of the page you add it to.
Add the ''title phrase'' parameter to search for something other than the entire pagename. :If you use the title-phrase parameter, do not use quotation marks, as they are ignored. (The ''intitle'' search parameter does not recognise quotation marks ''in'' the title anyway, but it does require them ''around'' a phrase, and these are added automatically.)
The default label is {{font color|blue|All pages with titles containing ''search term''}}. Add your own label in the ''label'' parameter to change this.
Use the parameter {{para|plural|yes}} to add a second search by appending "s" to the search term. Any other [[Plural form|plural forms]] will not be created with this parameter.
The parameter {{para|noquote|yes}} can be added to suppress quotation marks around the search term; this is required if the search term contains a wildcard: {{tlx|in title|visib*|noquote{{=}}yes}} produces: {{in title|visib*|noquote=yes}}
=== Examples === * On the page ''[[Wales]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Wales}}. * On the page ''[[Daytona 500]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Daytona 500}}. * {{tlc|In title|Babe Ruth}} produces {{In title|Babe Ruth}}. * {{tlc|In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}} produces {{In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=plural=yes}} produces {{In title|River|plural=yes}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=Pages with "River" in the title}} produces {{In title|River|Pages with "River" in the title}}.
=== Warnings === For print versions, this template leaves an empty bullet. See the talk page and the sandbox for resolution.
=== Related === To instead search for page titles that begin with a given word, or part of that word, use {{tl|look from}}.
== TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} <templatedata>{ "params": { "1": { "label": "Title phrase", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The phrase to generate a link for." }, "2": { "label": "Label", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The label to override the generated text." }, "plural": { "label": "Plural?", "type": "boolean", "autovalue": "yes", "description": "Outputs a second link for the plural version of the title phrase." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "Creates text and a link to an intitle: search on Wikipedia." }</templatedata>
== See also == * [[Template:Search link]]
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Search templates]] [[Category:Disambiguation and redirection templates]]
}}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> {{for|searching instead for page titles that '''begin''' with a word or part of a word|Template:Look from}} {{high risk}}
== Usage == In a See also section, use the following wikitext to create a link to a search for page titles which contain the given word or phrase: <nowiki />* {<nowiki />{ In title | ''title phrase'' | ''label'' }}
The default behavior of this template is searching for the entire [[Wikipedia:Pagename|pagename]] of the page you add it to.
Add the ''title phrase'' parameter to search for something other than the entire pagename. :If you use the title-phrase parameter, do not use quotation marks, as they are ignored. (The ''intitle'' search parameter does not recognise quotation marks ''in'' the title anyway, but it does require them ''around'' a phrase, and these are added automatically.)
The default label is {{font color|blue|All pages with titles containing ''search term''}}. Add your own label in the ''label'' parameter to change this.
Use the parameter {{para|plural|yes}} to add a second search by appending "s" to the search term. Any other [[Plural form|plural forms]] will not be created with this parameter.
The parameter {{para|noquote|yes}} can be added to suppress quotation marks around the search term; this is required if the search term contains a wildcard: {{tlx|in title|visib*|noquote{{=}}yes}} produces: {{in title|visib*|noquote=yes}}
=== Examples === * On the page ''[[:en:Wales|Wales]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Wales}}. * On the page ''[[Daytona 500]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Daytona 500}}. * {{tlc|In title|Babe Ruth}} produces {{In title|Babe Ruth}}. * {{tlc|In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}} produces {{In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=plural=yes}} produces {{In title|River|plural=yes}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=Pages with "River" in the title}} produces {{In title|River|Pages with "River" in the title}}.
=== Warnings === For print versions, this template leaves an empty bullet. See the talk page and the sandbox for resolution.
=== Related === To instead search for page titles that begin with a given word, or part of that word, use {{tl|look from}}.
== TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} <templatedata>{ "params": { "1": { "label": "Title phrase", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The phrase to generate a link for." }, "2": { "label": "Label", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The label to override the generated text." }, "plural": { "label": "Plural?", "type": "boolean", "autovalue": "yes", "description": "Outputs a second link for the plural version of the title phrase." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "Creates text and a link to an intitle: search on Wikipedia." }</templatedata>
== See also == * [[Template:Search link]]
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Search templates]] [[Category:Disambiguation and redirection templates]]
}}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> {{for|searching instead for page titles that '''begin''' with a word or part of a word|Template:Look from}} {{high risk}}
== Usage == In a See also section, use the following wikitext to create a link to a search for page titles which contain the given word or phrase: <nowiki />* {<nowiki />{ In title | ''title phrase'' | ''label'' }}
The default behavior of this template is searching for the entire [[Wikipedia:Pagename|pagename]] of the page you add it to.
Add the ''title phrase'' parameter to search for something other than the entire pagename. :If you use the title-phrase parameter, do not use quotation marks, as they are ignored. (The ''intitle'' search parameter does not recognise quotation marks ''in'' the title anyway, but it does require them ''around'' a phrase, and these are added automatically.)
The default label is {{font color|blue|All pages with titles containing ''search term''}}. Add your own label in the ''label'' parameter to change this.
Use the parameter {{para|plural|yes}} to add a second search by appending "s" to the search term. Any other [[Plural form|plural forms]] will not be created with this parameter.
The parameter {{para|noquote|yes}} can be added to suppress quotation marks around the search term; this is required if the search term contains a wildcard: {{tlx|in title|visib*|noquote{{=}}yes}} produces: {{in title|visib*|noquote=yes}}
=== Examples === * On the page ''[[:en:Wales|Wales]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Wales}}. * On the page ''[[Daytona 500]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Daytona 500}}. * {{tlc|In title|Babe Ruth}} produces {{In title|Babe Ruth}}. * {{tlc|In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}} produces {{In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=plural=yes}} produces {{In title|River|plural=yes}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=Pages with "River" in the title}} produces {{In title|River|Pages with "River" in the title}}.
=== Warnings === For print versions, this template leaves an empty bullet. See the talk page and the sandbox for resolution.
=== Related === To instead search for page titles that begin with a given word, or part of that word, use {{tl|look from}}.
== TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} <templatedata>{ "params": { "1": { "label": "Title phrase", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The phrase to generate a link for." }, "2": { "label": "Label", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The label to override the generated text." }, "plural": { "label": "Plural?", "type": "boolean", "autovalue": "yes", "description": "Outputs a second link for the plural version of the title phrase." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "Creates text and a link to an intitle: search on Wikipedia." }</templatedata>
== See also == * [[Template:Search link]]
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Search templates]] [[Category:Disambiguation and redirection templates]]
}}</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> {{for|searching instead for page titles that '''begin''' with a word or part of a word|Template:Look from}} {{high risk}}
== Usage == In a See also section, use the following wikitext to create a link to a search for page titles which contain the given word or phrase: <nowiki />* {<nowiki />{ In title | ''title phrase'' | ''label'' }}
The default behavior of this template is searching for the entire [[Wikipedia:Pagename|pagename]] of the page you add it to.
Add the ''title phrase'' parameter to search for something other than the entire pagename. :If you use the title-phrase parameter, do not use quotation marks, as they are ignored. (The ''intitle'' search parameter does not recognise quotation marks ''in'' the title anyway, but it does require them ''around'' a phrase, and these are added automatically.)
The default label is {{font color|blue|All pages with titles containing ''search term''}}. Add your own label in the ''label'' parameter to change this.
Use the parameter {{para|plural|yes}} to add a second search by appending "s" to the search term. Any other [[Plural form|plural forms]] will not be created with this parameter.
The parameter {{para|noquote|yes}} can be added to suppress quotation marks around the search term; this is required if the search term contains a wildcard: {{tlx|in title|visib*|noquote{{=}}yes}} produces: {{in title|visib*|noquote=yes}}
=== Examples === * On the page ''[[:en:Wales|Wales]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Wales}}. * On the page ''[[Daytona 500]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Daytona 500}}. * {{tlc|In title|Babe Ruth}} produces {{In title|Babe Ruth}}. * {{tlc|In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}} produces {{In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=plural=yes}} produces {{In title|River|plural=yes}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=Pages with "River" in the title}} produces {{In title|River|Pages with "River" in the title}}.
=== Warnings === For print versions, this template leaves an empty bullet. See the talk page and the sandbox for resolution.
=== Related === To instead search for page titles that begin with a given word, or part of that word, use {{tl|look from}}.
== TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} <templatedata>{ "params": { "1": { "label": "Title phrase", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The phrase to generate a link for." }, "2": { "label": "Label", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The label to override the generated text." }, "plural": { "label": "Plural?", "type": "boolean", "autovalue": "yes", "description": "Outputs a second link for the plural version of the title phrase." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "Creates text and a link to an intitle: search on Wikipedia." }</templatedata>
== See also == * [[Template:Search link]]
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Search templates]] [[Category:Disambiguation and redirection templates]]
}}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> {{for|searching instead for page titles that '''begin''' with a word or part of a word|Template:Look from}} {{high risk}}
== Usage == In a See also section, use the following wikitext to create a link to a search for page titles which contain the given word or phrase: <nowiki />* {<nowiki />{ In title | ''title phrase'' | ''label'' }}
The default behavior of this template is searching for the entire [[Wikipedia:Pagename|pagename]] of the page you add it to.
Add the ''title phrase'' parameter to search for something other than the entire pagename. :If you use the title-phrase parameter, do not use quotation marks, as they are ignored. (The ''intitle'' search parameter does not recognise quotation marks ''in'' the title anyway, but it does require them ''around'' a phrase, and these are added automatically.)
The default label is {{font color|blue|All pages with titles containing ''search term''}}. Add your own label in the ''label'' parameter to change this.
Use the parameter {{para|plural|yes}} to add a second search by appending "s" to the search term. Any other [[Plural form|plural forms]] will not be created with this parameter.
The parameter {{para|noquote|yes}} can be added to suppress quotation marks around the search term; this is required if the search term contains a wildcard: {{tlx|in title|visib*|noquote{{=}}yes}} produces: {{in title|visib*|noquote=yes}}
=== Examples === * On the page ''[[:en:Wales|Wales]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Wales}}. * On the page ''[[Daytona 500]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Daytona 500}}. * {{tlc|In title|Babe Ruth}} produces {{In title|Babe Ruth}}. * {{tlc|In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}} produces {{In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=plural=yes}} produces {{In title|River|plural=yes}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=Pages with "River" in the title}} produces {{In title|River|Pages with "River" in the title}}.
=== Warnings === For print versions, this template leaves an empty bullet. See the talk page and the sandbox for resolution.
=== Related === To instead search for page titles that begin with a given word, or part of that word, use {{tl|look from}}.
== TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} <templatedata>{ "params": { "1": { "label": "Title phrase", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The phrase to generate a link for." }, "2": { "label": "Label", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The label to override the generated text." }, "plural": { "label": "Plural?", "type": "boolean", "autovalue": "yes", "description": "Outputs a second link for the plural version of the title phrase." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "Creates text and a link to an intitle: search on Wikipedia." }</templatedata>
== See also == * [[Template:Search link]]
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Search templates]] [[Category:Disambiguation and redirection templates]]
}}</includeonly> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23458359115|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> {{for|searching instead for page titles that '''begin''' with a word or part of a word|Template:Look from}} {{high risk}}
== Usage == In a See also section, use the following wikitext to create a link to a search for page titles which contain the given word or phrase: <nowiki />* {<nowiki />{ In title | ''title phrase'' | ''label'' }}
The default behavior of this template is searching for the entire [[Wikipedia:Pagename|pagename]] of the page you add it to.
Add the ''title phrase'' parameter to search for something other than the entire pagename. :If you use the title-phrase parameter, do not use quotation marks, as they are ignored. (The ''intitle'' search parameter does not recognise quotation marks ''in'' the title anyway, but it does require them ''around'' a phrase, and these are added automatically.)
The default label is {{font color|blue|All pages with titles containing ''search term''}}. Add your own label in the ''label'' parameter to change this.
Use the parameter {{para|plural|yes}} to add a second search by appending "s" to the search term. Any other [[Plural form|plural forms]] will not be created with this parameter.
The parameter {{para|noquote|yes}} can be added to suppress quotation marks around the search term; this is required if the search term contains a wildcard: {{tlx|in title|visib*|noquote{{=}}yes}} produces: {{in title|visib*|noquote=yes}}
=== Examples === * On the page ''[[:en:Wales|Wales]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Wales}}. * On the page ''[[Daytona 500]]'', {{tlc|In title}} produces {{In title|Daytona 500}}. * {{tlc|In title|Babe Ruth}} produces {{In title|Babe Ruth}}. * {{tlc|In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}} produces {{In title|Somersworth|Search for Somersworth-related articles}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=plural=yes}} produces {{In title|River|plural=yes}}. * {{tlc|1=In title|2=River|3=Pages with "River" in the title}} produces {{In title|River|Pages with "River" in the title}}.
=== Warnings === For print versions, this template leaves an empty bullet. See the talk page and the sandbox for resolution.
=== Related === To instead search for page titles that begin with a given word, or part of that word, use {{tl|look from}}.
== TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} <templatedata>{ "params": { "1": { "label": "Title phrase", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The phrase to generate a link for." }, "2": { "label": "Label", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "The label to override the generated text." }, "plural": { "label": "Plural?", "type": "boolean", "autovalue": "yes", "description": "Outputs a second link for the plural version of the title phrase." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "Creates text and a link to an intitle: search on Wikipedia." }</templatedata>
== See also == * [[Template:Search link]]
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata -->
}}</includeonly> <noinclude> [[Category:Search templates]] [[Category:Disambiguation and redirection templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24938264069|time triggered pipeline]]
